ai_strategy_lead.name: UH AI Planning Group / AISC
ai_strategy_lead.title: Public AI strategy or initiative lead / hub identified from cited sources

ai_strategy.summary: University of Hawaii at Manoa has a public AI posture anchored in UH AI Planning Group / AISC. The cited sources show activity across responsible-use guidance, teaching resources, research or infrastructure, and AI literacy or workforce preparation.

key_provisions: Use AI transparently and follow course, research, workplace and academic-integrity expectations.
Do not enter confidential, regulated, sensitive, unpublished or protected institutional data into unapproved public AI tools.
Human users remain accountable for accuracy, bias, copyright, privacy and final decisions based on AI output.
ai_policy.summary: University of Hawaii at Manoa publishes AI policy or guidance with a comprehensive stance: AI use is allowed or actively explored, but bounded by privacy, ethics, academic integrity, compliance and human-review expectations.

bodies.name: UH AI Planning Group / AISC
bodies.scope: Public AI strategy, guidance, teaching, research or technology coordination.

governance_structure.summary: AI governance at University of Hawaii at Manoa is evidenced through UH AI Planning Group / AISC and the cited policy or strategy sources. Public sources did not identify a separate chief AI officer during this sweep.

initiatives.title: UH ITS AI services catalog and Google AI tools
initiatives.description: UH ITS publishes an AI services page listing institution-supported or available tools such as Gemini, NotebookLM, Zoom AI Companion, Otter.ai, Adobe AI Assistant, Grammarly, Panopto AI Smart Chapters, and Microsoft Copilot.
initiatives.significance: The catalog replaces a generic platform gap with named tools and an institutional route for approved use.

investment.description: Public AI catalog does not disclose contract values or active-user counts.

collaborations.description: Vendors are named in the UH ITS AI tools listing.

initiatives.policy_implications: Tool use is framed through UH guidance for responsible AI and data handling.

initiatives.title: Bow AI chatbot for UH data governance
initiatives.description: UH launched Bow, an AI chatbot for the Data Governance Office that helps users find information about data-governance policies, definitions, and resources.
initiatives.significance: This is a concrete student/staff-facing operational chatbot signal rather than a generic AI-resource page.

investment.description: Public launch article does not disclose build or operating cost.

collaborations.description: Public article does not identify an external vendor.

regulatory_context.summary: No binding state higher-education AI policy was identified during this sweep; institutional guidance is the main policy evidence.

narrative_summary: University of Hawaii at Manoa has a documented AI posture across policy, teaching, research, infrastructure or access. The central public signal is UH AI Planning Group / AISC, supported by policy or guidance sources that encourage responsible use while emphasizing privacy, academic integrity, ethics and human oversight. The institution also shows curriculum or AI literacy activity and at least one research, center, infrastructure or applied innovation signal. Its maturity is strongest where public sources show durable structures: committees, centers, portals, degree programs, supercomputing, enterprise tools, or workforce initiatives. The gaps are also important: this sweep did not find a standalone chief AI officer in public sources for most assigned institutions, and several initiatives do not disclose exact budgets, user counts or implementation metrics. Overall, the evidence supports a medium-confidence R1 profile with enough public signals to compare the institution across governance, teaching, research, enterprise tools, workforce and equity dimensions.
